pages.raw_text:
The time for fling the announce
ments of candidates for the two
places on the school board, to be
filed at the election December 5,
expired yesterday. There are but
three candidates in the field, A. w.
Davis and w. S. Gilbert, present
members of the board, and II. l..
Hughes, editor of the Labor World.


Mr.. Hughes has the endorsement
of the labor organizations of the
city, which have come to the con-
clusion that they are entitled to
representation on the school board.
A strong fight will be made for
Hughes, as he is considered in
every way qualified to become an
active and useful member of the
board.


Women have a right to vote In
the school election. and many are
registering and preparing to exer-
else their privilege. The rgistra-
ton books are open at the Elk
drug store, 424 Sprague avenue,
where they will remain the rest of
this week. Saturday is the last day
of registration for the school cleo
ton.


1t will not be necessary for vot-
ers who registered for the general
election this month to register
again for the school election.
